summary: Sprint 3 testing — Backend 30/30 pytest GREEN (added 11 new tests for /api/my-issues, project CRUD with delete cascading to tasks, owner-only invites CRUD, public invite preview, idempotent invite accept with membership persistence, WS unauth=4401 close, and WS authenticated presence + task.created broadcast). Frontend pages My Issues, Projects (create+delete), Settings (workspace+members+invite links), and /join/{code} (logged-in and anonymous) ALL render correctly with proper data-testids. Drag-and-drop on Kanban board WORKS — dragged CORE-256 from Backlog to In Progress, status persisted via PATCH (verified by hard reload). Copy invite link writes to clipboard and toggles 'Copied' checkmark. HOWEVER: real-time WebSocket sync from the BROWSER is BROKEN — backend WS broadcasts confirmed working (pytest client receives presence + task.created), but the React client never connects: 'WebSocket is closed before the connection is established' warning, board-presence indicator never appears, and tasks created from a second browser context do NOT show on the first board without manual reload.

integration_issues.issue: The browser WS connection fails to establish: console warning 'WebSocket connection to wss://...api/ws/board/{id} failed: WebSocket is closed before the connection is established'. Result: (a) [data-testid=board-presence] never appears even when a second authenticated client is connected on the same board, (b) tasks created from another browser/context do NOT appear in real time — page1 must be reloaded to see them. Backend WS endpoint itself works fine — proven by pytest using ?token=<jwt> query param. The frontend hook relies purely on cookies for WS auth, which appears to be stripped/not forwarded by the Emergent ingress during the WSS upgrade.

action_items: Fix browser WS auth: add an endpoint like GET /api/auth/ws-token that returns a short-lived JWT in a JSON body (not httpOnly), and update useBoardSocket.js to fetch it and connect as `${wsUrl}?token=${t}`. The backend already supports the `?token=` fallback (server.py:_authenticate_ws lines 657-666) — only the frontend needs the change.
Alternative fix: in set_jwt_cookie, also write a non-httpOnly companion cookie (e.g. access_token_ws) with the same JWT for WSS handshake — keeps current useBoardSocket.js cookie-only flow.
After fix, verify board-presence avatar stack appears with 2+ connected clients, and that task.created/updated/deleted broadcasts update the board without reload.
critical_code_review_comments: useBoardSocket.js relies on cookie auth for WS — fragile through reverse proxies/CDNs. Backend already exposes a ?token= fallback; the frontend should use it (low effort, high impact).
server.py is now 1059 lines — overdue for splitting into routers/{auth,workspaces,projects,tasks,comments,invites,ws}.py; not a blocker.
DELETE /api/projects deletes the project but does NOT delete its comments (only tasks). For demo data this is fine; for production consider cascading comments delete too.

accept_invite auto-deletes the user's previously-owned empty workspace (server.py:830-837). This is intentional MVP behavior but should be revisited when multi-workspace support is added — flagged in code comment already.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (added 11 Sprint 3 tests: TestMyIssues, TestProjectsCRUD, TestInvites, TestWebSocket)

success_rate.frontend: ~88% — all pages and DnD work; WS real-time sync is the only broken piece

test_credentials: demo@taskflow.com / demo1234 — working. Test users created on the fly: test_<hex>@example.com / secret123.
seed_data_creation: Several new TEST_-prefixed tasks created during pytest run on Core Platform (auto-cleaned by their own tests where possible; a few stragglers like TEST_NI_*, TEST_ws_* may remain — harmless). Multiple test workspaces auto-removed by accept_invite logic. 4 invite links remain in LinearSync from test runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes WS auth in useBoardSocket.js: (1) reload board, (2) open Network tab — WS handshake should be 101, (3) open same board in incognito with another login — verify [data-testid=board-presence] appears on both pages with 2 avatars, (4) create a task via API from one session and verify it appears in the other without reload. Backend pytest is the source of truth — 30/30 must remain green after any fix.
rca of the issue: RCA — Frontend WS connection fails: The hook /app/frontend/src/hooks/useBoardSocket.js (line 29) opens `new WebSocket(${REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL}/api/ws/board/${projectId})` and relies on the browser auto-attaching the httpOnly access_token cookie on the WSS handshake. The Emergent k8s ingress (or an intermediate proxy) appears to NOT forward Cookie headers during the WSS Upgrade, so the backend's _authenticate_ws receives no cookies, returns None, and closes with code 4401. Browser surfaces this as a generic 'WebSocket is closed before the connection is established'. Reproduction: open https://flow-dev-1.preview.emergentagent.com/app/board/proj_coreplatform as demo, observe console warning; query selector [data-testid=board-presence] returns null even with a second authenticated client on the same board. Mitigation (low risk): the backend already supports `?token=<jwt>` as a query-param fallback (server.py lines 657-666). Either (a) expose a JSON endpoint that returns the JWT and append it as a query param in useBoardSocket.js, or (b) set a non-httpOnly cookie (companion of access_token) that the browser will pass on the WS upgrade — option (a) is more secure.
